item.title: Beyond the Score: Why “Explain Your Thinking” Matters
item.description: Learn how Explain Your Thinking helps educators use AI to make student reasoning visible and improve feedback, assessment, and intervention.

item.title: Mapping the Noncredit Data Taxonomy 3.0 to the CTDL: A New Benchmark Model for Noncredit Education Data | Credential Engine
item.description: A new recommended benchmark and publishing template from Credential Engine helps states, institutions, and agencies bring noncredit education data into the credential transparency ecosystem. Credential Engine has released a new...

item.title: Khanmigo’s First Chapter Changed How I Think About AI: A note from Sal Khan
item.description: Sal Khan reflects on Khanmigo's first chapter, what AI tutoring taught us, and how Khan Academy is building better support for students.

item.description: Credential Engine’s recently launched Issuer Identity Registry (IIR) marks an important step toward strengthening trust in digital credentials. By enabling organizations to verify the identity of credential issuers, the IIR...

item.description: Credential Engine released a Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking an experienced partner to help scale CTDL xTRA, an open source, AI-assisted platform developed by Credential Engine to transform publicly available...

item.title: Best Free TEKS-Aligned Science Resources for Texas Teachers This Year

item.description: Explore 7 ways Texas math teachers can use Khan Academy’s free TEKS-aligned math courses, now spanning grades 3–12 with new Statistics and Financial Mathematics resources.

item.title: The Best Early Learning Apps for Kids: A Parent and Teacher Guide
item.description: Looking for the best early learning apps for kids? We reviewed 9 top-rated, ad-free options — including Khan Academy Kids, PBS Kids, Pok Pok, and more — so parents and teachers don't have to guess.

item.title: Five Ways Khan Academy Is Making Student Practice More Motivating
item.description: Gem Challenges build on the spirit of LearnStorm, helping students see their progress, earn Gems, and work together toward shared class goals.

item.title: Built in the Open: How Pilot Districts Shaped the Reimagined Khan Academy
item.description: See how pilot districts shaped the reimagined Khan Academy with integrated Khanmigo support, clearer learning paths, and tools that keep students motivated.

item.title: Kodi On The Go: Bring Kodi Bear on Your Summer Adventures
item.description: Meet your child's new summer travel buddy. Print, color, and cut out Kodi Bear and bring her on every road trip, beach day, and backyard adventure this summer. Share your photos with #KodiOnTheGo.

item.title: The Duality of May: Why Global Accessibility Awareness Day Hits Differently
item.description: Explore how Khan Academy celebrated Global Accessibility Awareness Day through reflection, firsthand feedback, a no-mouse challenge, and accessibility fixes.

item.description: Georgia is setting a new standard for how states connect education and workforce. Credential transparency is part of that vision. Yesterday, Georgia’s Governor signed HB 1302, the Education and Workforce Strategy Act, taking an...

item.description: Forsyth Technical Community College and Credential Engine will work to leverage the power of transparent credential data to strengthen the region’s education and training market.

item.title: Edtech Security: From Shared Practice to Concrete Action | 1EdTech
item.description: A few weeks ago I wrote that cybersecurity in our sector is a shared responsibility , and that the honest question underneath every incident is not "which vendor failed?" but "who is responsible for what?" The 1EdTech Board of Directors unanimously supported that response and the feedback from members, institutional leaders, and technology providers was immediate and constructive. The consistent message: agreement on the principle, and a desire to see it turned into specifics.

item.title: From Opening the Door to Belonging in the Building: Why LTI 1.3 Matters | 1EdTech
item.description: From Opening the Door to Belonging in the Building: Why LTI 1.3 Matters | 1EdTech

item.title: Together Is the New Standard: Building the Infrastructure for What Comes Next | 1EdTech
item.description: We just wrapped up an empowering week at the 2026 Learning Impact Conference in San Francisco, where one thing was clear: education technology is entering a new era, and the infrastructure behind it must evolve just as quickly.

item.title: Aligning the Ecosystem: A Common Path Forward for Student Data | 1EdTech
item.description: A simpler way to manage roster data: The OneRoster support in the Ed-Fi ODS/API provides a consistent way to share data across systems, improving accuracy and reducing duplicate work.

item.title: Interoperability and the Screen Time Debate: Finding the Right Balance | 1EdTech
item.description: Screen time has become one of the most debated topics in education. Parents, educators, and policymakers often ask: How much is too much? But a more useful question is: How meaningful is that time?

item.title: Higher Education & The Trump Administration
item.description: Executive orders, agency memos and directives, news stories and other resources on the Trump administration's first weeks in office.

item.title: NOVA President Anne Kress to Serve as ACE Board Chair
item.description: ACE’s membership also elected Robert W. Iuliano​, president of Gettysburg College, as Boa​rd vice-chair, and also elected a slate​​ of new Board members, all of whom also will begin their terms on Oct. 1, 2026.

item.title: New ACE Guidance Helps Colleges Navigate Ethics Rules for Campus Visits
item.description: ACE has released a new issue brief clarifying federal ethics rules for hosting members of Congress and executive branch officials on campus, as colleges and universities navigate today's complex political climate.

item.title: 2025 Freshman Survey Shines Light on Views of Entering College Students
item.description: The 2025 CIRP Freshman Survey features key insights about how entering college students are approaching higher education in a changing context in which questions about cost, value, well-being, and future opportunities are becoming even more important.
